strengths: Elite pre-snap diagnostic speed and scheme processing; among the best ILB minds in the 2026 class. Walk-on story encapsulates the APEX character ideal: earned scholarship, earned starting role, earned first-round consideration through merit alone. Outstanding motor and effort consistency, elevates in high-leverage run fits and blitz packages.
red_flags: Athletic profile is below the elite tier for the position â€” lacks the closing speed and man-coverage athleticism to match up with slot receivers in dime packages. UCLA competition level (Pac-12/Big Ten transition) was not consistently SEC-quality, limiting SOS-adjusted production confidence. Projection as a two-down thumper without continued coverage development.
capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2 top; Walk-On Flag +0.5 round upside; CRUSH premium confirmed; Tier A confidence

strengths: Generationally unique two-way player with elite CB and WR production in the same season â€” a feat not accomplished at Power 5 level in decades. 9.5 athleticism profile combines top-end speed (sub-4.4 projection), elite jump ball skills, and exceptional change-of-direction. Two-Way Premium reflects genuine draft capital elevation: whichever side he plays, he profiles as a top-10 pick at that position.
red_flags: NFL teams must commit to one position; transition risk is real regardless of skill set. Two-way college workload means fewer reps in pure CB technique fundamentals (press technique, trail technique, jam footwork) compared to a single-position prospect. COMPRESSION gap reflects his elite balance across all archetypes rather than a concern â€” no dominant archetype because all are elite.
capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 1-10; Two-way premium; capital reflects top-side CB/WR position; Tier A confidence

strengths: Elite touch and accuracy from the pocket, NFL-ready release mechanics, and exceptional ability to navigate pressure and extend plays within his frame. Strong statistical output at Colorado against Power 4 competition demonstrates adaptability beyond his JSU baseline. Process as a passer, specifically his pre-snap identification and quick release reads, is genuinely high-floor.
red_flags: System dependency is the primary concern â€” Colorado's offense was architecturally designed around Sanders' skill set under Deion Sanders' bespoke scheme. Tier C confidence reflects legitimate unknowns: independent coaching relationship (Deion factor reduces confidence in c2/c3 baseline accuracy), limited scheme-diverse reps, and questions about translating production to an NFL system he did not design. RB athleticism profile limits scramble/extend threat value significantly. Capital carries Â±1 round Tier C range.
capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2 top) / R2; Tier C â€” Â±1 round range, monitor; system dependency discount applied

strengths: Young and physically imposing OT with elite athleticism for the position â€” long arms, exceptional anchor strength, and outstanding short-area burst that stresses interior defenders. Missouri background provided meaningful SEC-adjacent competition, and Membou dominated in both run and pass protection. Developmental trajectory is among the best in this class: already dominant at a young age with clear ceiling remaining.
red_flags: COMPRESSION gap indicates he profiles well in multiple archetypes (athletic projection and complete prospect) without a dominant singular identity â€” a positive signal for this class. Still developing elite hand technique and combat consistency in pass rush, which will be stress-tested in the NFL. Limited power conference playoff depth at Missouri reduces SOS confidence.
capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32 / R1 Top depending on team need; Tier A confidence

strengths: Elite run blocker with outstanding leverage, power, and contact-seeking mentality â€” every NFL run-first coordinator's ideal interior blocker. Four-year starter at Georgia with consistent dominance in SEC competition against NFL-caliber defensive linemen. Excellent competitive toughness and effort tail that elevates every snap.
red_flags: Athletic profile for an OG is solid but not exceptional; limited to run-heavy scheme alignments in full exploitation. Pass protection against elite speed rushers off the edge is his biggest projection concern â€” tendency to overextend hand placement under pressure. SOLID archetype gap is clean but not elite, reflecting a clear fit without the upside ceiling of the COMPRESSION tier.
capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2 top; Tier A confidence

strengths: Well-rounded TE prospect with above-average athleticism and reliable hands in contested catch situations. Texas system provided meaningful opportunities against Big 12 pass rushers and coverage. Effective in both seam routes and in-line blocking, making him a viable two-way TE at the NFL level. TWEENER gap reflects balance between receiving-first and complete archetypes.
red_flags: Not an elite separator â€” relies on size and contested-catch ability rather than route precision or YAC creation. Blocking technique needs refinement at the point of attack against NFL-caliber defensive ends. His overall profile projects as a quality starter but not a top-5 TE in this class. Draft capital reflects good but not elite upside.
capital_adjusted: R2 / R3 top; Tier A confidence

strengths: Good short-area quickness and burst behind pads, effective in zone-read and inside zone concepts. Georgia production reflects good efficiency when healthy and playing behind an elite offensive line. Legitimate pass-catching ability from the backfield is a positive NFL-era trait.
red_flags: NO_FIT archetype gap reflects a profile that doesn't dominate any specific RB archetype â€” not elite enough to anchor as a feature back, not specialized enough to be a true satellite/receiving back, not physical enough as a between-the-tackles specialist. PVC of 0.70 (lowest positional premium) reflects market reality. Tier B confidence due to transfer context and inconsistent production volume. DEVELOPMENTAL tier is appropriate given the current trait-to-production ceiling alignment; projection requires scheme-specific development.
capital_adjusted: Day 3 / R4-R5 range; Tier B â€” Â±0.5 round range; RB positional discount applied

strengths: Elite athletic profile for the safety position â€” outstanding speed, range, and zone coverage ability that projects to premium box safety or single-high roles. South Carolina production showed consistent effort and playmaking against SEC competition. Physical profile and processing speed make him a legitimate early-round safety in the post-Ed Reed era of the position.
red_flags: SOS Gate applied: South Carolina schedule strength in 2025 did not include consistent elite competition, creating uncertainty in the production-quality floor. v_production discounted from raw assessment by -1.5 to reflect this. Tier B confidence due to SOS limitations. COMPRESSION gap reflects a complete safety profile without a singular dominant trait â€” athleticism leads but processing and versatility are nearly equal; this is a positive signal, not a concern.
capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2 top; SOS Gate note â€” production confidence Tier B; Â±0.5 round range

strengths: Elite athleticism and pass rush burst off the edge â€” combination of long arms, get-off speed, and bend around the corner that projects as a legitimate starter in any scheme. Boston College production was consistently high against ACC competition with multiple double-digit sack seasons. Developmental trajectory is strong; still adding counter moves to complement elite first-step.
red_flags: TWEENER gap reflects legitimate split between athletic projection and production machine archetypes â€” not a negative at this composite score, but signals he has not yet become the complete pass rusher his athleticism demands. BC competition level (ACC) is a step below SEC/Big Ten elite, limiting SOS confidence at the margins. Run stop and coverage deployment in complex NFL schemes requires developmental investment.
capital_adjusted: R1 (late) / R2 top; Tier A confidence; development premium flagged

strengths: Elite production from the interior at Ohio State against Big Ten competition â€” consistently penetrated against NFL offensive linemen and generated wins both against the run and in pass rush. Exceptional competitive toughness; does not take snaps off, high motor in pursuit. Athleticism profile for an IDL is above average with a quick first step and leverage advantage.
red_flags: SOLID archetype gap (not COMPRESSION) reflects a player who is clearly a Production Machine without elite versatility or developmental upside ceiling. IDL PVC of 0.90 reflects positional market reality. Route to elite production in the NFL requires continued counter-move development â€” currently relies heavily on power and leverage rather than diverse pass rush plan.
capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2; Tier A confidence; production-led profile

strengths: Elite center intelligence â€” outstanding pre-snap communication, protection call identification, and line calls make him a natural for NFL green-dot center duties. Pittsburgh background developed a complete center skill set across both run and pass protection. Processing speed at the center position is among the best in the 2026 class; veteran coaches consistently praise his film study.
red_flags: Athletic profile is serviceable but below elite for the position â€” lacks the burst to pull effectively in stretch schemes or reach block against explosive 3-techs. C PVC of 0.80 reflects positional market reality; centers rarely command premium capital regardless of quality. Production at Pitt (ACC competition) did not consistently stress-test against Big Ten or SEC interior talent.
capital_adjusted: R2 / R3 top; Tier A confidence; C positional discount reflected

strengths: Dominant run blocker at Georgia against elite SEC competition â€” powered the Bulldogs' run game with consistent positional dominance at the point of attack. Outstanding physical profile for the center position with excellent anchor strength and hand power. SEC championship experience provides elite competition floor confidence.
red_flags: Processing and protection call intelligence is good but below elite â€” projects more as a run-first center than a pure green-dot communicator, which limits upside in pass-heavy schemes. C PVC of 0.80 reflects market reality. Development in pass protection technique (hand replacement, set mechanics under speed) is the key NFL translation variable.
capital_adjusted: R2 / R3; Tier A confidence; C positional discount; production-led profile
